title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents raise engineering practice to the system level rather than eliminating it. She contends that core engineering disciplines like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain essential when designing agentic systems.
writeup: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering work up to the system level rather than removing it. The talk holds that core disciplines, systems thinking, decomposition, and state management, stay essential when designing agentic and multi-agent systems. The takeaway: building agentic systems is an engineering problem, and separation of concerns and deliberate design matter more than writing the code itself.
